CREATE TABLE test_table (id Int32, created_at DateTime) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id TTL created_at + INTERVAL 7 DAY;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at) VALUES (1, now()), (2, now() - INTERVAL 8 DAY);
SELECT * FROM test_table;
OPTIMIZE TABLE test_table;
ALTER TABLE test_table MODIFY TTL created_at + INTERVAL 14 DAY;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE created_at < now() - INTERVAL 10 DAY;
OPTIMIZE TABLE test_table;
ALTER TABLE test_table DROP TTL;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at) VALUES (3, now() - INTERVAL 5 DAY);
OPTIMIZE TABLE test_table;
SELECT * FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE test_table_2 (id Int32, created_at DateTime) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO test_table_2 (id, created_at) VALUES (1, now()), (2, now() - INTERVAL 15 DAY);
ALTER TABLE test_table_2 ADD COLUMN updated_at DateTime;
ALTER TABLE test_table_2 MODIFY TTL updated_at + INTERVAL 3 DAY;
OPTIMIZE TABLE test_table_2;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id FROM test_table_2 WHERE created_at < now() - INTERVAL 10 DAY) AS subquery;
ALTER TABLE test_table_2 DROP COLUMN updated_at;
SELECT count() FROM test_table_2 WHERE created_at < now() - INTERVAL 12 DAY;
CREATE TABLE test_table_3 (id Int32, created_at DateTime) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id TTL created_at + INTERVAL 1 DAY;
INSERT INTO test_table_3 (id, created_at) VALUES (1, now() - INTERVAL 2 DAY);
OPTIMIZE TABLE test_table_3;
SELECT * FROM test_table_3 WHERE created_at < now() - INTERVAL 3 DAY;
